title: Podman exec fails.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Unable to exec on a container. 
podman exec -it nextcloud ls
Error: exec failed: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:105: adding pid 3049948 to cgroups caused \"failed to write 3049948 to cgroup.procs: write /sys/fs/cgroup/machine.slice/libpod-9b47d3d27e186652d4db7589ba303129e8776d2f1d905c4c2b5d1fb41361c0a2.scope/cgroup.procs: device or resource busy\"": OCI runtime error
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.podman start nextcloud

2. podman ps --filter name=nextcloud
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                         COMMAND         CREATED       STATUS                PORTS   NAMES
9b47d3d27e18  localhost/nextcloud:18.0.0-1  /usr/sbin/init  3 months ago  Up About an hour ago          nextcloud


3. podman exec -it nextcloud ls
Error: exec failed: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:105: adding pid 3049948 to cgroups caused \"failed to write 3049948 to cgroup.procs: write /sys/fs/cgroup/machine.slice/libpod-9b47d3d27e186652d4db7589ba303129e8776d2f1d905c4c2b5d1fb41361c0a2.scope/cgroup.procs: device or resource busy\"": OCI runtime error

**Describe the results you received:** I got an exec error as mentioned above.


**Describe the results you expected:** The output of ls is expected output.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** This does not happen on all containers. I think it started to happen when i changed from cgroups v1 to cgroups v2.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Oct  7 11:21:20 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.1.1-12.fc33.x86_64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** Physical machine

comments.body: Does the problem only happen with this one container, or can you reproduce with other containers?
Just this container. All other containers work fine.
I have a feeling you need to remove all containers created with cgroups V1 to fix this problem.

> I have a feeling you need to remove all containers created with cgroups V1 to fix this problem.

That is a disruptive solution. There are twenty other containers running on this server. 
Can you please explain why you feel that removing other containers would be the solution? Also why wont just recreating this container be a solution? I could consider it even though it defeats the purpose of using containers for portability.

Can you do a `podman inspect` of that container and check the OCIRuntime - I suspect that this container is still using runc on cgroups v2, from the error message. I'm a tad surprised it started at all, given that... @rhatdan @lsm5 How new is our runc on F33?

@MrErr If we can keep the containers, that is fine, I am just concerned about changing the cgroups from under the container and expecting it to still work.  But @mheon might be correct about the change from runc to crun.
That container's still on `runc`. I'm feeling like this could be a runc + cgroups v2 bug given the relative immaturity of the support there.

I switched back to cgroups v1 and all containers seem to be working fine.

What should be the method to switch to cgroups v2?

We added a `--runtime` flag to `podman system migrate` to force all containers to switch to a new OCI runtime - so, with all containers stopped on a fresh boot in v2 mode, run `podman system migrate --runtime crun` and then start the containers. They should be migrated to the crun runtime and work as expected.
Since we have a solution, I am closing the issue.
